claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 21 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 21 years old, they are above the usual age limit for Child Benefit, which typically applies to children under 16. However, there is an exception for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent) or approved training, and if so, has this been continuous since before they turned 19?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Let me check the eligibility rules to give you an accurate assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the eligibility rules, I now have enough information to make a determination about your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

**Alex (age 21) – Not Eligible**

Child Benefit has specific age requirements:
- Children must be **under 16 years old**, OR
- **Under 20 years old** and still in approved education or training

Since Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in any education or training, they exceed the maximum age for Child Benefit eligibility. Even if Alex were to start education or training now, at 21 they would still be over the age limit.

I understand this may not be the outcome you were hoping for. If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you have or become responsible for a younger child), you may be able to claim Child Benefit at that time.

Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old, which exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and is not currently in any education or training, they do not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit. The rules state you can only qualify if the child is under 16, or under 20 and in approved education or training. Alex meets neither of these conditions.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit eligibility requires the child to be either under 16, or under 20 and in approved education or training. As Alex is 21 and not in education, they are too old to qualify for Child Benefit under any circumstances.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old, which exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and is not currently in any education or training, they do not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit. The rules state you can only qualify if the child is under 16, or under 20 and in approved education or training. Alex meets neither of these conditions.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit eligibility requires the child to be either under 16, or under 20 and in approved education or training. As Alex is 21 and not in education, they are too old to qualify for Child Benefit under any circumstances.
